{{Short description|2001 film awards ceremony}} {{Use dmy dates|date=April 2022}} {{Infobox film awards | number = 54 | award = British Academy Film Awards | date = 25 February 2001 | site = [[Odeon Luxe Leicester Square]] | host = [[Stephen Fry]]
[[Mariella Frostrup]] | best_film = '''''[[Gladiator (2000 film)|Gladiator]]''''' | best_british = '''''[[Billy Elliot]]''''' | best_actor = '''[[Jamie Bell]]''' | best_actor_film = ''[[Billy Elliot]]'' | best_actress = '''[[Julia Roberts]]''' | best_actress_film = ''[[Erin Brockovich (film)|Erin Brockovich]]'' | most_wins = '''''[[Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon]]''''' and '''''[[Gladiator (2000 film)|Gladiator]]''''' (4) | most_nominations = '''''[[Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon]]''''' and '''''[[Gladiator (2000 film)|Gladiator]]''''' (14) | last = 53rd | next = 55th }} The '''54th British Academy Film Awards''', more commonly known as the [[BAFTAs]], took place on 25 February 2001 at the [[Odeon Luxe Leicester Square|Odeon Leicester Square]] in [[London]], honouring the best national and foreign films of [[2000 in film|2000]]. Presented by the [[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]], accolades were handed out for the best feature-length film and documentaries of any nationality that were screened at [[Cinema of the United Kingdom|British cinemas]] in 2000.{{cite web |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/entertainment/1184232.stm|title='Gladiator' conquers the Baftas|publisher=[[BBC]]|date=25 February 2001 |access-date=28 August 2023}} [[Ridley Scott]]'s ''[[Gladiator (2000 film)|Gladiator]]'' won [[BAFTA Award for Best Film|Best Film]], while [[Ang Lee]] won [[BAFTA Award for Best Direction|Best Director]] for ''[[Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon]]''. ''[[Billy Elliot]]'' was voted Outstanding British Film of 2000; the film's lead actor, [[Jamie Bell]], won [[BAFTA Award for Best Actor in a Leading Role|Best Actor in a Leading Role]]. In addition, [[Julia Roberts]] won [[BAFTA Award for Best Actress in a Leading Role|Best Actress in a Leading Role]] for her portrayal of [[Erin Brockovich]] in ''[[Erin Brockovich (film)|Erin Brockovich]]'', [[Benicio del Toro]] won [[BAFTA Award for Best Actor in a Supporting Role|Best Actor in a Supporting Role]] for his role in ''[[Traffic (2000 film)|Traffic]]'', and [[Julie Walters]] won [[BAFTA Award for Best Actress in a Supporting Role|Best Actress in a Supporting Role]] for her role in ''Billy Elliot''.{{cite magazine|url= https://ew.com/article/2001/02/26/gladiator-and-crouching-tiger-win-big-baftas/|title=''Gladiator'' and ''Crouching Tiger'' win big at the BAFTAs|first=Sandra|last=P. Angulo|magazine=[[Entertainment Weekly]]|date=February 26, 2001|access-date= December 16, 2023 |archive-date= December 16, 2023|archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20231216161815/https://ew.com/article/2001/02/26/gladiator-and-crouching-tiger-win-big-baftas/|url-status=live}}{{cite web |url=https://www.cnn.com/2001/SHOWBIZ/Movies/02/25/baftas.winners/index.html|title='Gladiator' wins BAFTA's Best Film|publisher=[[CNN]] |date=25 February 2001|access-date=28 August 2023}} The nominees were announced on 31 January 2001.{{cite web |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2001/jan/31/baftas2001.baftasfilm|title='Gladiator', 'Crouching Tiger' do battle in Bafta nominations|work=[[The Guardian]]|date=31 January 2001|access-date=28 August 2023}} [[Stephen Fry]] and [[Mariella Frostrup]] co-hosted the ceremony together, marking the first time the ceremony had two hosts in over 10 years. ==Winners and nominees== [[File:Ang Lee - 66ème Festival de Venise (Mostra).jpg|thumb|right|150px|[[Ang Lee]], Best Director winner and Best Film Not in the English Language co-winner]]
